channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Iranian Women's Soccer Team Stands in Silent Protest During Asian Cup National Anthem
item.description: During the opening match of the 2026 AFC Women's Asian Cup at Cbus Super Stadium in Gold Coast, the Iranian women's national soccer team chose not to sing their national anthem, remaining silent as a ...

item.title: FG Starts February Salary Payment and Settles Part of Wage Arrears for Federal Workers
item.description: The Office of the Accountant-General of the Federation (OAGF) don start to pay February 2026 salaries to all Federal Government workers wey dey funded by treasury. This follow after plenty complaints ...

item.title: Anthony Albanese marks three decades in Parliament
item.description: Anthony Albanese has reached a significant milestone, marking 30 years as a member of the Australian Parliament. Over this period, he has been involved in numerous political initiatives, policy debate...

item.title: PNP increases policewomen in barangays to assist VAWC victims
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) is stepping up its efforts to address the rising cases of violence against women and their children (VAWC) by deploying more policewomen in barangays across the co...

item.title: Laikipia North MP Builds Peace and Education Through Amani School
item.description: Sarah Korere, an MP from the marginalised Ndorobo community in Laikipia North, has made significant strides in promoting peace, education, and equity in a region long affected by ethnic clashes, cattl...

item.title: Assessing the 2026 FIFA World Cup Amid Political and Human Rights Concerns
item.description: The 2026 FIFA Men’s World Cup will be the largest in history, hosted across Canada, Mexico, and the United States from June 11 to July 19, with 48 countries participating. While preparations have been...

item.title: Philippines, US, and Japan Conduct Joint Air Patrols in Northern Philippine Waters
item.description: The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) announced that joint air patrols with the United States and Japan reached as far north as the Bashi Channel, a key waterway separating northern Luzon from Tai...

item.title: Spurs' Luke Kornet Requests Hawks Cancel Strip Club-Themed Promotional Night to Respect Women
item.description: Luke Kornet, center for the San Antonio Spurs and 2024 NBA champion with the Boston Celtics, publicly called on the Atlanta Hawks to cancel their upcoming 'Magic City Night' scheduled for March 16 aga...

item.title: MARINA urges Philippine vessels to increase security due to US-Iran tensions
item.description: The Maritime Industry Authority (MARINA) has issued a directive for all Philippine-registered ships to adopt heightened security measures following escalating tensions between the United States and Ir...

item.title: Geo News clarifies hacking incident, unauthorised message not linked to channel
item.description: Geo News has reported that during the past 24 hours, certain malicious elements attempted to hack the channel's satellite transmission on Paksat, targeting the prime-time 9 pm bulletin, which attracts...

item.title: Guidance for Parents and Teachers on Safe Generative AI Use by Children
item.description: Generative AI (GenAI) has rapidly moved from a novelty to a daily tool for many adults and children, appearing in schoolwork, entertainment, and social media. While useful, unsupervised AI use can exp...

item.title: Students and Staff Protest Against Alleged Land Takeover of FUT Minna Bosso Campus by Niger State Government
item.description: For Monday, students and staff of the Federal University of Technology, Minna, Niger State, carry out peaceful protest against Governor Umaru Bago’s alleged plan to take over Bosso Campus. Demostrator...

item.title: Senate Approves Bill Moving BARMM Elections to September 2026
item.description: COTABATO CITY – Presidential Peace Adviser Carlito Galvez Jr. expressed his appreciation for the Senate, led by Senate President Vicente “Tito” Sotto III, after they approved a bill that resets the fi...

item.title: International Flights Delayed at Pakistan Airports Due to Middle East Tensions
item.description: KARACHI: At least 11 international flights have been stranded at different airports across Pakistan for the past three days due to escalating tensions in the Middle East following the US and Israel at...

item.title: African Students Learn Conflict Resolution Skills Through CEFA Peace Project
item.description: Secondary school students from Tanzania, Kenya, and Mozambique recently took part in a training organized under a special peace-building initiative by the Italian NGO CEFA. The training aims to equip ...

item.title: New Neonatal Unit Opens at Kwimba District Hospital to Improve Newborn Care
item.description: On 1st March 2026, a brand new neonatal care facility officially opened at Kwimba District Hospital in Mwanza Region, Tanzania. The building is modern and built to international standards, designed to...

item.title: Families of Children Abused at North London Nursery to Meet Education Secretary Over Safeguarding Failures
item.description: Families whose children were abused by former Bright Horizons nursery employee Vincent Chan in West Hampstead, north London, are scheduled to meet Education Secretary Bridget Phillipson to push for re...

item.title: Senate raises concerns over counties not fully operationalising municipalities
item.description: The Senate County Public Investments and Special Funds Committee has expressed serious concerns over the slow pace at which many counties in Kenya are implementing the provisions of the Urban Areas an...

item.title: Ebonyi Businessman Claims Ex-Gov Umahi Took His Land Under Road Expansion Pretext
item.description: For real, matter dey hot for Ebonyi State as businessman, Mr. Okay Emmanuel Nnolum, don accuse former governor David Umahi, now Minister of Works, say him dey use road dualisation project around Dr. A...

item.title: India and Canada Sign $2.6 Billion Uranium Supply Agreement and Strengthen Strategic Partnership
item.description: In a significant development, India and Canada have revitalised their bilateral relationship with a landmark $2.6 billion deal for supplying 22 million pounds of uranium to India between 2027 and 2035...
